MASTER CALENDAR MOTION ON FEB-06-2013 IN DEPT. 206, DEFENDANT MAUREEN KENNEY'S APPLICATION PENDING FOR WAIVER OF COURT FEES AND COSTS CONTINUED FROM FEB-06-2013 TO FEB-13-2013, 9:00 AM IN DEPT. 206 IN ORDER FOR PLAINTIFF TO FILE AN AMENDED APPLICATION. MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT OF DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT FILED BY DEFENDANT MWAMBA, EVEREST

LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT MAUREEN KENNEY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO MARCH 4, 2013 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302, ON THE COURT'S MOTION. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

MASTER MOTION CALENDAR ON FEB-13-2013 IN 206, HEARING ON APPLICANT/DEFENDANT MAUREEN KENNEY'S WAIVER OF COURT FEES AND COSTS. APPEARANCE BY APPLICANT. DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL MAUREEN KECK ALSO APPEARED. THE COURT GRANTED THE APPLICATION IN WHOLE. ORDER SIGNED, AND ENDORSED-FILED COPY MAILED TO APPLICANT. JUDGE: CYNTHIA M. LEE; CLERK: JIROQUE; MATTER NOT REPORTED. (206)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MAUREEN KENNEY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO PLEAD, IF HE CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, FACTS SUFFICIENT TO STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FRAUDULENT CONVEYANCE INCLUDING WHAT PROPERTY HAS BEEN TRANSFERRED AND HOW. TO THE EXTENT THAT PLAINTIFF SEEKS TO HAVE THE COURT VOID A PENDING FAMILY LAW MATTER, THIS COURT LACKS JURISDICTION TO DO SO. PLAINTIFF SHOULD FILE AN APPLICATION FOR JOINDER IN THE FAMILY LAW CASE UNDER CRC SECTION 5.24. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON II, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-16-2013 Off Calendar

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-04-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. MICHAEL S. KECK, TEL. NO. 861-2267 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT MAUREEN KENNEY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to plead, if he can do so in good faith, facts sufficient to state a cause of action for fraudulent conveyance including what property has been transferred and how. To the extent that Plaintiff seeks to have the Court void a pending family law matter, this Court lacks jurisdiction to do so. Plaintiff should file an application for joinder in the family law case under CRC section 5.24. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: A. James Robertson II, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/AJR
